symptoms: Diamond or eye-shaped lesions on leaves
Gray or white center with dark brown borders
Lesions on leaf collar, neck, and panicle
Severe: neck rot causes entire panicle to die
treatment: Apply Tricyclazole or Isoprothiolane fungicide immediately
Remove and burn infected plant material
Avoid excess nitrogen — use split application
prevention: Treat seeds with fungicide before planting

symptoms: Small oval to circular brown spots on leaves
Dark brown borders with gray/white centers
Infected leaves turn yellow and die
causes: Nutrient-poor soils — nitrogen and potassium deficiency
treatment: Correct soil nutrients with balanced fertilizer
prevention: Use certified seeds treated with fungicide
Maintain soil fertility — nitrogen and potassium

symptoms: Water-soaked lesions from leaf tips or margins
Lesions turn yellow to white as they enlarge
Affected leaves dry out and die
Yellow bacterial ooze in humid conditions
causes: Heavy rain or flooding causing leaf wounds
treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide to slow spread
prevention: Maintain proper spacing for air circulation

symptoms: Leaves uniformly green with no spots or lesions
No discoloration, wilting, or abnormal growth
prevention: Monitor regularly for early disease signs
